get.summary: Get Api V1 Classes
get.description: Auto-generated documentation for GET /api/v1/classes. Supports cursor pagination through the `limit`, `sort`, and `cursor` query parameters. The exact total hit count is returned in the `X-Total-Count` response header unless `include_total=false`, and the next page cursor is returned in the `X-Next-Cursor` response header.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return. Defaults to 100. The server clamps values above 250.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Comma-separated sort fields. Cursor pagination uses the requested sort order and appends a stable tie-breaker automatically.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned in the X-Next-Cursor response header from a previous page. Supply it unchanged to fetch the next page.

parameters.name: include_total
parameters.description: Whether to execute an exact count query and return X-Total-Count. Defaults to true; set false on latency-sensitive requests that do not need the count.

200.description: Classes matching optional query filters The response body contains the current page items as a JSON array. Use the `X-Total-Count` header for the exact number of matching results. Use the `X-Next-Cursor` header, when present, to request the next page.

X-Next-Cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. This header is omitted when there are no more results.

X-Page-Limit.description: Effective page size used by the server after applying the configured default and maximum.

X-Total-Count.description: Exact total number of results matching the current filter set, independent of cursor pagination. Omitted when include_total=false.

get.description: Name-addressed alias for the class endpoint. The explicit by-name segment always treats numeric-looking values as names.

delete.summary: Delete a class by name
delete.description: Name-addressed alias for class deletion. The original name and resolved ID are rechecked under the transactional row lock.

404.description: Class not found or concurrently renamed

patch.description: Name-addressed alias for class update. Authorization is bound to the resolved class, and the original name plus resolved ID are rechecked under the transactional row lock.

404.description: Class not found or concurrently renamed

get.summary: Aggregate objects in a class by class name
get.description: Name-addressed alias for permission-scoped object aggregation. Numeric-looking class names remain names. Normal object filters and up to two enabled shared or owned personal computed filters are applied before grouping.

parameters.name: group_by
parameters.description: One to three repeated ordered dimensions: name, description, collection_id, created_at, updated_at, json_data.<comma-separated-path>, computed.shared.<key>, or computed.personal.<key>

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Aggregate ordering: dimensions.asc, dimensions.desc, object_count.asc, or object_count.desc

200.description: Permission-scoped aggregated object counts. Value states distinguish value, JSON null, a missing JSON path, and an unavailable computed result.

400.description: Invalid filter, dimension, path, sort, cursor, or computed selector

413.description: An aggregate value is too large for a replay-safe cursor, or source snapshots or externally authorized intermediate aggregates exceed their memory bounds

get.summary: List objects in a class by class name

parameters.name: sort

parameters.name: cursor

404.description: Class/object pair not found or concurrently renamed

patch.summary: Apply JSON Patch to object data by object name
patch.description: Fully name-addressed alias for the atomic object-data JSON Patch endpoint. Class names are globally unique, object names are unique within their class, and both names are URL percent-decoded. The server resolves the natural key for authorization, then requires that same object ID, class ID, class name, and object name when taking the transactional row lock so a concurrent rename cannot redirect the patch. Numeric-looking names are always treated as names on this route. Every `path` and `from` remains relative to the root of the raw `data` document.

parameters.name: class_name
parameters.description: Globally unique class name, URL percent-encoded as needed

parameters.name: object_name
parameters.description: Class-scoped object name, URL percent-encoded as needed

requestBody.description: RFC 6902 operation array. Supports add, remove, replace, move, copy, and test. Limited to 1,000 operations and 128 pointer segments per path/from.

200.description: Updated object, or the unchanged object for a no-op patch

400.description: Malformed JSON, invalid JSON Patch structure or bounds, or a result PostgreSQL JSONB cannot represent

404.description: Class-name/object-name pair not found or the object was concurrently renamed

406.description: Final patched data fails the class JSON Schema

409.description: A patch operation failed, including a failed test operation; no change was persisted

413.description: JSON Patch request or resulting object data exceeds its resource limits

500.description: Persistence, computed-field materialization, or event emission failed and the transaction was rolled back

patch.summary: Patch Api V1 Classes By Class Id By Object Id
Apply JSON Patch to object data
patch.description: Atomically applies an RFC 6902 JSON Patch document to the object's raw `data` document. Every `path` and `from` is an RFC 6901 JSON Pointer relative to the root of `data`; an empty path replaces the complete document. Operations are evaluated against the latest row-locked data. A successful change updates schema validation, computed-field materialization, history, timestamps, and the object update event in one transaction. A patch that produces no data change returns the current object without changing its timestamp or emitting an update event.

requestBody.description: RFC 6902 operation array. Supports add, remove, replace, move, copy, and test. Limited to 1,000 operations and 128 pointer segments per path/from.

200.description: Updated object, or the unchanged object for a no-op patch

400.description: Malformed JSON, invalid JSON Patch structure or bounds, or a result PostgreSQL JSONB cannot represent

406.description: Final patched data fails the class JSON Schema

409.description: A patch operation failed, including a failed test operation; no change was persisted

413.description: JSON Patch request or resulting object data exceeds its resource limits

500.description: Persistence, computed-field materialization, or event emission failed and the transaction was rolled back

get.description: Auto-generated documentation for GET /api/v1/remote-targets/{target_id}/events. Supports cursor pagination through the `limit`, `sort`, and `cursor` query parameters. The exact total hit count is returned in the `X-Total-Count` response header unless `include_total=false`, and the next page cursor is returned in the `X-Next-Cursor` response header.

parameters.name: occurred_after
parameters.description: Optional lower occurred_at bound, RFC3339 or YYYY-MM-DD

parameters.name: occurred_before
parameters.description: Optional upper occurred_at bound, RFC3339 or YYYY-MM-DD

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return. Defaults to 100. The server clamps values above 250.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Comma-separated sort fields. Supported fields: id, occurred_at

parameters.name: include_total
parameters.description: Whether to execute an exact count query and return X-Total-Count. Defaults to true; set false on latency-sensitive requests that do not need the count.

200.description: Visible remote target audit events The response body contains the current page items as a JSON array. Use the `X-Total-Count` header for the exact number of matching results. Use the `X-Next-Cursor` header, when present, to request the next page.

X-Next-Cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page. This header is omitted when there are no more results.

X-Page-Limit.description: Effective page size used by the server after applying the configured default and maximum.

X-Total-Count.description: Exact total number of results matching the current filter set, independent of cursor pagination. Omitted when include_total=false.

BackupHistory.description:
Privileged, restore-only table snapshots. In backup version 3, each section
name and row shape corresponds to the PostgreSQL table restored from it.
These are versioned disaster-recovery internals, not portable import data.

include_history.description:
Full-system backups include durable audit, task, delivery, and temporal
history by default. Set this explicitly to false only when the eventual
restore is intended to reset that history.

BackupState.description: Exact logical rows used by the destructive full-system restore path.

ClientConfig.description:
Public configuration values that API consumers need to use the service correctly.

Keep this projection deliberately small. Adding a value here makes it available without
authentication through the client configuration endpoint.

default_page_limit.description: Number of items returned when a paginated request omits its limit.

max_page_limit.description: Largest effective page size used for a client request.

class_id.description: Required by the personal preview route and ignored on class-scoped routes.

acquisition_wait_time_ms.description: Cumulative time spent waiting for connections, in milliseconds.

acquisitions_timed_out.description: Cumulative acquisitions that exceeded the acquisition timeout.

acquisitions_waited.description: Cumulative acquisitions that waited for a connection.

available_connections.description: Remaining capacity: idle connections plus capacity to create connections.

connections_closed_broken.description: Cumulative connections discarded because they were broken.

connections_closed_idle_timeout.description: Cumulative connections closed after reaching the idle timeout.

connections_closed_invalid.description: Cumulative connections discarded after checkout validation failed.

connections_closed_max_lifetime.description: Cumulative connections closed after reaching their maximum lifetime.

connections_created.description: Cumulative connections established by the pool.

idle_connections.description: Established connections currently waiting in the pool.

in_use_connections.description: Established connections currently checked out of the pool.

last_vacuum_time.description: Most recent vacuum timestamp among user tables.

max_connections.description: Configured maximum number of connections in this process-local pool.

pending_acquisitions.description: Connection acquisitions currently waiting to complete.

total_connections.description: Connections currently managed by this process-local pool.

EventSinkID.description: Identifier wrapper for an event sink.

EventSubscriptionFilter.description:
Optional additional fan-out filter for an event subscription.

`entity_types` and `actions` remain first-class subscription fields because
they drive catalog validation and coarse fan-out selection. This filter
narrows those matches by stable event-envelope fields. Empty or omitted
fields match all events for that dimension.

output_expired.description:
True when output was produced but has since passed its retention window. Distinguishes an
expired export from one that was never generated (both have `output_available = false`).

NewCollectionWithAssignee.description:
A new collection, with an assignee. Used for creating new collection entries
into the database and assign all permissions to the group given as group_id.

This wraps the NewCollection struct and uses the group_id to grant all permissions
to the group in a single transaction.

ObjectDataPatchDocument.description: RFC 6902 operations applied relative to the root of an object's raw data document. Supports add, remove, replace, move, copy, and test. The resulting document is limited to 2 MiB and 64 nested containers, with a bounded cumulative application-work budget.

PrincipalCollectionPermissions.description:
A principal's direct permission rows on a single collection, broken down by the
group that grants them.

PrincipalMemberResponse.description: Public representation of a group member (a principal of either kind).

PrincipalSettings.description:
An object-only JSON document containing a principal's local preferences.

Values below the document root may be any JSON type. The private
representation keeps callers from constructing an invalid non-object root.

PrincipalToken.description:
A persisted bearer token, keyed to a principal, with a full lifecycle. The
`token` field stores the HMAC hash, never the raw value.

PrincipalTokenMetadata.description: Public, hash-free projection of a token for listing.
